fault_hypothesis: The expiration check on line 68 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`). This causes entries to be considered valid when the current time is exactly equal to the expiration time, but they should be treated as expired.

constraints.intent: The check for expiration fails at the exact boundary; this condition holds for all passing tests but is false for all failing tests.
A cached route should only be returned if the current time is strictly before its effective expiration.
The effective expiration time, which may include a tolerance, should not be earlier than the stored expiration time.
The function must return either a string route or None, matching its type hint Optional[str].
